mission.title: Install and Configure Rancher / RKE2
mission.description: Rancher is a complete container management platform that simplifies running Kubernetes clusters across various environments. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to manage multiple Kubernetes clusters with ease. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and potential complexity in initial setup.

steps.title: Install the Rancher CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Rancher CLI, which is necessary for managing Rancher from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/rancher/cli/releases/download/v2.14.0/rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf rancher-linux-amd64-v2.14.0.tar.gz

sudo mv rancher /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Rancher CLI tarball.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the Rancher CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using RKE2 with the specified version and node count.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

sudo systemctl enable rke2-server.service

sudo systemctl start rke2-server.service

```

> Install RKE2 on the server.
> Enable the RKE2 server service to start on boot.
> Start the RKE2 server.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with the new cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/rke2/rke2.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> Copy the RKE2 kubeconfig to your home directory.
> Change ownership of the kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are ready and functioning.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/examples/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rancher/rancher/master/examples/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring tools.
> Deploy autoscaling configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rancher
uninstall.description:
This step removes Rancher and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

sudo systemctl stop rke2-server.service

sudo rm -rf /var/lib/rancher

```

> Stop the RKE2 server.
> Remove Rancher data directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rancher
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > rancher-backup.yaml

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

curl -sfL https://get.rke2.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current Rancher resources.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Run the upgrade script for RKE2.
> Verify that all nodes are back to 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly set up and points to the right cluster certificate.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: xxxxx'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Node status shows 'NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to insufficient resources or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node's resource allocation and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to start container'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the container runtime or the container image.

**Fix:**
Inspect the container logs for more details using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced UI features for cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.9, ensure to migrate any legacy authentication configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
